title: PointAction: 3D Points as Universal Action Representations for Robot Control
summary: PointAction introduces a framework that bridges video-generative models and robot control by jointly predicting RGB frames and dynamic 3D point maps, creating embodiment-agnostic action representations. A foundation video model first produces temporally consistent RGB and XYZ pointmaps; a diffusion-based decoder then translates point dynamics into executable robot commands, providing a structured interface between visual predictions and low-level actions. The two-stage approach enables cross-task and cross-embodiment transfer with minimal action supervision, demonstrated on real robot arms (YAM and xArm7) and the RoboCasa365 simulation benchmark.
